designated_agency_details.name_of_designated_agency: Harbourside Family & Community Services (Designated Carer Agency)
designated_agency_details.information_you_sought_to_be_changed:
I requested that several pieces of information recorded about me on the Carers Register be corrected and updated, as they are now out of date and in one case factually incorrect:

1. PRIMARY HOME ADDRESS
The Register still lists my previous address as:
"Unit 9, 33 Station Parade, Meadowfield NSW 2147".
I moved out of this property in March 2023 following the end of my lease and separation from my former partner. I have been residing at my current address for over two years:
"27 Kestrel Street, Lakeview NSW 2155".
I provided the agency with a copy of my current lease agreement (in my name, commencing 2023-03-10) and a recent electricity bill dated 2025-08-30 showing this address, to verify the change. I requested that the Register be amended so that my home address reflects my current and stable residence in Lakeview, as this is where I provide care and where home visits and support services should be directed.

2. CONTACT PHONE NUMBER
The Register still lists my old mobile number ("(04) 12 345 678"), which has been disconnected since late 2022 after I changed providers for cost reasons. I now use a landline as my primary contact:
"(02) 9458-3276".
I also provided my current mobile number as a secondary contact in my written request, but I emphasised that the landline is the most reliable, particularly during the evenings when I am usually at home with the children in my care. I asked that the Register be updated so that caseworkers and after-hours services can reach me without delays caused by calling the wrong number.

3. HOUSEHOLD COMPOSITION / MARITAL STATUS
The Register still shows me as "married" and residing with my then-husband, Daniel Harrington. We legally separated in November 2022 and our divorce was finalised on 2024-05-06. Daniel has not resided with me since early 2023 and has no involvement in my current caring arrangements. I requested that the Register be updated to show my status as "single" and to remove Daniel from the household members list, as this has caused confusion when caseworkers have assumed there are two adult carers regularly present in the home when in reality I am the sole day-to-day carer.

4. AVAILABILITY AND CAPACITY TO CARE
When I was first authorised, I worked full-time outside the home, and the Register notes limited weekday availability. As of January 2024 I have shifted to part-time employment (three days per week, school hours) specifically to better accommodate my caring responsibilities. I provided a letter from my employer confirming my changed hours. I requested that the Register be updated to reflect that I am now available for school drop-offs and pick-ups on Mondays and Fridays and available for most appointments outside of my work hours.

5. CARER TRAINING RECORD
The Register currently does not show completion of two training modules I undertook through the agency in 2024: "Trauma-Informed Care – Intermediate" (completed 2024-03-12) and "Supporting Children with Autism and ADHD" (completed 2024-08-19). I attached copies of my certificates of completion and requested that these be added to my record, as this information is important when matching children to carers and when caseworkers are considering my suitability for placements with particular needs.

6. EMERGENCY CONTACT DETAILS
The Register lists my mother, Margaret Lewis, as my primary emergency contact. Unfortunately, my mother passed away in February 2023. I requested that the Register be updated to list my sister, "Laura Lewis", as my primary emergency contact instead, with her current phone number and address. I provided Laura’s written consent and her updated contact details in the original request. This is crucial so that, in the event of an emergency involving a child in my care, the agency is able to reach an actual, living relative who is aware of and willing to assist with my caring role.

Overall, my request was to have all of the above items corrected so the Carers Register accurately reflects my current living situation, contact details, support network, and training, and so that caseworkers are not relying on information that is several years out of date.

outcome_of_request.details_of_why_the_designated_agency_did:
When I contacted Harbourside Family & Community Services and submitted my written request on 2025-09-18, I followed up twice by email and once by phone. On 2025-10-07, I received a formal written response from the agency advising that they were not able to make all of the requested changes on the Carers Register at that time.

The reasons they provided were as follows:

1. ADDRESS AND HOUSEHOLD COMPOSITION – REQUIREMENT FOR FURTHER INTERNAL VERIFICATION
The agency acknowledged that I had provided a copy of my lease agreement and a recent utility bill showing my current address at 27 Kestrel Street, Lakeview. However, they stated that under their internal policy, changes to a carer’s primary address and household composition (including removal of a former spouse from the record) must be verified by a home visit and updated home safety assessment before the Carers Register can be amended. They indicated that due to current staffing pressures and a backlog of home visits, they could not prioritise my home visit for at least another three to four months. Until that visit is completed, they advised they would continue to use my old address and household details in the Register, even though they acknowledged that this information is out of date. They did not provide a clear timeframe in writing, only saying that I would be contacted "in due course" to schedule the visit.

2. CONTACT PHONE NUMBER – PARTIAL UPDATE BUT NOT REFLECTED ON THE REGISTER
During a follow-up call with the intake officer, I was told that they had updated my contact number in their internal case management system but that the Carers Register, which they described as a "separate, centralised database", had not yet been updated due to an "IT freeze" while they transition to a new system. The letter I received stated that while staff would use my updated landline number when contacting me, the central Register would still show my old disconnected mobile number until the system changeover was completed. They were unable to give a specific date for when the system changeover would occur, only indicating it was expected "sometime early next year".

3. MARITAL STATUS AND REMOVAL OF FORMER SPOUSE – REQUEST FOR ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTATION
Although I provided a copy of my divorce order finalised on 2024-05-06 and explained that my ex-husband, Daniel Harrington, has not lived with me since 2023, the agency’s response letter stated that they require either:
- a statutory declaration from me confirming that Daniel no longer resides at my address and has no caring role in relation to any current or future placements, and
- confirmation from Daniel himself (or evidence of reasonable attempts to obtain this) that he does not wish to be listed as a carer or household member.

They explained that until this additional documentation is provided and processed, they would not remove Daniel’s name from the household section of the Register, even though he is not involved in my caring responsibilities and has not been present during any recent home visits. This is particularly frustrating because it means the Register still implies that there are two adults in the household providing care, which is not the case.

4. TRAINING RECORD – DELAY DUE TO ADMINISTRATIVE BACKLOG
The agency did accept that I had completed the two additional training modules ("Trauma-Informed Care – Intermediate" and "Supporting Children with Autism and ADHD") and acknowledged receipt of my certificates. However, they advised that updates to training records on the central Carers Register are currently processed only quarterly and that the next update cycle had already been finalised at the time my request was received. As a result, they said my new training modules would be "noted on file" but would not appear on the formal Register until at least the next quarterly update, and possibly later if their data entry backlog continued. This means that anyone viewing the Register in the meantime will still see an incomplete picture of my skills and training.

5. EMERGENCY CONTACT – PRIVACY AND CONSENT CONCERNS
Even though I supplied written consent from my sister, Laura Lewis, to be listed as my emergency contact, the agency’s letter stated that they had concerns about whether the consent form I provided met their current privacy and information-sharing requirements. They said the form I used was an older template and that they have since updated their consent documentation. They insisted that Laura must complete a new, agency-specific consent form in person or via a witnessed video call before they can officially change the emergency contact details on the Register. Until that happens, the Register continues to list my late mother, Margaret Lewis, as my primary emergency contact, which is obviously inaccurate and distressing.

6. GENERAL SYSTEM AND POLICY CONSTRAINTS
Overall, the tone of the response suggested that the main reason for not making the requested changes promptly was a combination of system limitations, internal policy requirements, and staff workload rather than any dispute about the truthfulness of the information I provided. In our phone conversation, the contact person, Rebecca Collins, informally acknowledged that the current details on the Register are "not ideal" and "a bit out of date", but she maintained that she was bound by the agency’s procedures and could not override them. She encouraged me to submit the additional documents (such as the statutory declaration and the updated consent form) and to wait for the next round of system updates and data entry.

In summary, while the agency informally accepted that much of the information I provided was accurate, they have not yet actually amended the Carers Register. The Register still shows my old address, my old phone number, my ex-husband as a current household member, incomplete training records, and my deceased mother as my emergency contact. Their refusal or inability to make timely changes has practical consequences for my caring role, including potential delays in communication during emergencies, misunderstandings about the level of support in my household, and possible missed opportunities for placements that would benefit from my updated training and increased availability.
